title: Unblocker: Question
description: Generates a provocative question to interrogate your work-in-progress.
entries: What is your protagonist most afraid someone will find out?
Who in this scene wants something they will not admit?
What does your character believe that the story is about to prove wrong?
If this scene were deleted, what would the reader actually lose?
Who has the most power here, and who thinks they do?
What is the lie your protagonist tells themselves to sleep at night?
Whose absence is shaping this scene more than anyone present?
What would have to be true for the villain to be right?
What does your character want, and what do they need that they cannot see?
Where in this chapter are you being polite instead of honest?
What is the worst thing that could happen right now, and why won't you let it?
Who changes in this scene, even a little, and how would we know?
What memory is your protagonist trying not to have?
If your character got exactly what they asked for, would it destroy them?
What is everyone in the room pretending not to notice?
What did your protagonist do before the story started that they still owe for?
Whose version of events are we getting, and what are they leaving out?
What small kindness could cost a character everything here?
What is the question this whole story is quietly trying to answer?
Where are you telling the reader how to feel instead of trusting the scene?
What would your character never do, and how do you get them to do it anyway?
Who is the one person your protagonist cannot lie to, and where are they?
What is at stake if nothing changes in this scene?
What does your character love that the plot is going to make them choose against?
Whose approval is your protagonist secretly writing every action toward?
What is true in this scene that no one is allowed to say out loud?
If you asked the antagonist to describe your hero, what would sting most?
What does your character do when they think no one is watching?
Where is the story too comfortable, and who could you send in to ruin it?
What has your protagonist been running from since page one?
Which character is telling the truth in a way no one wants to hear?
What would break if your most reliable character turned out to be wrong?
What is the smallest object in this scene, and why does it matter?
Who is your protagonist trying to become, and who are they afraid they already are?
What promise is about to be broken, and does anyone remember it was made?
Where does the scene end for the characters but keep going for you?
What does silence mean between these two people right now?
If your protagonist won this argument, would they lose something worse?
What are you protecting your reader from that you should let them feel?
Whose story would this be if you moved the camera one seat over?
